explanation: The natural language sentence describes a conditional transition with specified parameters for the resulting state. The first part, 'The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window,' implies a global (G) property: if the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode) and the patient fails to trigger a breath within the apnea lag time (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then eventually (F) it will transition to PCV mode (PCVMode). This initial translation is G((PSVMode & breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime) -> F PCVMode). The second part, 'The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode,' refines the target state of the transition. It specifies that when PCVMode is reached due to this switch, it must be accompanied by the apnea backup parameters: respiratory rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), target inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and I:E ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). Thus, the 'F PCVMode' part is refined to 'F (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)'. Combining these elements yields the final LTL formula.

The main condition for the transition is 'if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window', which maps to the atomic proposition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime'. This condition is applied while the system is in 'PSVMode' (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode), indicating the starting point of the transition. Thus, the antecedent of the implication is 'PSVMode & breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime'. The consequent describes the outcome: 'The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur', meaning 'PCVMode' (Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode) will eventually be true ('F PCVMode'). Furthermore, this switch happens with 'apnea' detection and specific parameters defined for the apnea backup mode. These parameters are 'respiratory rate', 'target inspiratory pressure', and 'I:E', which map to 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' respectively. These must all be true when 'PCVMode' is entered due to this apnea condition. Therefore, the eventual state is a conjunction of 'PCVMode', 'apnea', and these three parameters. Combining these, the formula becomes G((PSVMode & breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime) -> F (PCVMode & apnea & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)).
